examples.title: DBMS Query Processing Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates how a user query passes through different DBMS components
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Query_Execution(userQuery)
BEGIN
  // Step 1: Query Parser
  parsedQuery = PARSE(userQuery)
  
  // Step 2: Query Optimizer
  optimizedPlan = OPTIMIZE(parsedQuery)
  
  // Step 3: Execution Engine
  result = EXECUTE(optimizedPlan)
  
  // Step 4: Buffer Manager retrieves data
  IF data NOT in buffer THEN
    LOAD_FROM_DISK()
  ENDIF
  
  RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how DBMS processes a query through parsing, optimization, and execution stages before returning results to the user.

examples.title: Simple DBMS Architecture Simulation
examples.description: Python class demonstrating DBMS components working together
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates key DBMS components: Data Dictionary for schema, Transaction Manager with commit/rollback, and basic query processing.

examples.title: File System vs DBMS Storage Management
examples.description: C program comparing manual file handling with DBMS-like approach
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates DBMS buffer management - how DBMS caches data pages in memory to reduce disk I/O, tracks dirty pages, and uses pin counting for concurrency control.
examples.timeComplexity: O(B) where B is buffer size

examples.title: Transaction Management in DBMS
examples.description: Java class demonstrating ACID properties of transactions
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates transaction management with ACID properties: Atomicity (commit/rollback), Isolation (locking), Durability (write-ahead logging), and Consistency through proper transaction handling.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of operations
